title: Retry Safety, Replayability, Backoff, Jitter, and Retry Budgets · Browser and Node Client Syst… · Contract Lens: Retry Budget Amplification
summary: A high-traffic web SDK retries selected reads under 429 and 503 without replaying one-shot streams or synchronizing every browser tab. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts retry budget amplification increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch bounded retry and replay decision engine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Limit total attempts across nested callers and prevent retry storms, duplicate effects, and load amplification. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Interpret Retry-After for 429 or 503, bound unreasonable values, and reconcile it with the caller deadline. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Predict Retry Budget Amplification · Theory Retry Budget Amplification
steps.instruction:
theory case — TypeScript Fetch evidence model sees one reset, Retry-After:5, then 503 while a nested helper also retries. Prove method and body replayability, cap attempts and elapsed budget, apply deterministic jitter, and prevent duplicate or amplified work. Competency probe: Limit total attempts across nested callers and prevent retry storms, duplicate effects, and load amplification. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
